title: Compiling big design issue(s)...
body:
I'm trying to compile a relatively big design in flat mode (i.e. not --hierarchical), and run into following "clang" compiler failure with "verialted" cpp files. It finished verliating, and failed at C++ compile step.

sirius_tb__2.cpp:5:10: fatal error: sorry, this include generates a translation unit too large for Clang to process.
#include "sirius_tb.h"

"sirius_tb" is top level RTL module name. 
The design took ~ 17hrs and 470Gbyte of memory, with latest EPYC 7571  to verliate it.  And generate ~ 10Gbyte of cpp files ! And shame couldn't compile it ;)

Verilator 4.101 devel rev v4.100-26-gf8ab30d1
Ubuntu clang version 11.0.0-++20200928063537+eb83b551d3e-1~exp1~20200928164152.107

I'll be happy to provide more details, if its helpful. 

FYI - when used ( "with" --hierarchical) design will compile in respectable 4min, 9Gbyte memory.  However, it can only be run at single threaded mode (slow sim time). 

Any thoughts/comments appreciated.
